title: [Unsupported] force/release faulty case
body:
I was trying out force/release and faced an error saying this:

```%Error: transaction.sv:8:20: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                                                                                                  : ... note: In instance '$unit::transaction'
    8 |   randc bit [31:0] signal_1;
      |                    ^~~~~~~~~~~~
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.035 for more assistance.
%Error: transaction.sv:9:20: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                                                                                                  : ... note: In instance '$unit::transaction'
    9 |   randc bit [31:0] signal_2;
      |                    ^~~~~~~~~~~~
%Error: transaction.sv:7:20: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                                                                                                  : ... note: In instance '$unit::transaction'
    7 |   randc bit [31:0] signal_3;
      |                    ^~~~~~~~
%Error: transaction.sv:1:1: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                                                                                                 : ... note: In instance '$unit::transaction'
    1 | class child_transaction extends transaction;
      | ^~~~~
%Error: transaction.sv:15:17: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                                                                                                   : ... note: In instance '$unit::transaction'
   15 |    signal_1 inside {32'h00000000,32'hffff_ffff};
      |                 ^~~~~~
%Error: transaction.sv:19:17: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                                                                                                   : ... note: In instance '$unit::transaction'
   19 |    signal_2inside {32'h00000000,32'hffff_ffff};
      |                 ^~~~~~
%Error: transaction.sv:23:17: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                                                                                                   : ... note: In instance '$unit::transaction'
   23 |    signal_3 inside {32'h00000000,32'hffff_ffff};
      |                 ^~~~~~
%Error: transaction.sv:27:13: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                                                                                                   : ... note: In instance '$unit::transaction'
   27 |    signal inside {32'h00000000,32'hffff_ffff};
      |             ^~~~~~
%Error: driver.sv:74:24: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                                                                                              : ... note: In instance '$unit::driver'
   74 |           gen2driv.get(driv_trans);
      |                        ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~
%Error: scoreboard.sv:20:19: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                                                                                                  : ... note: In instance '$unit::scoreboard__pi4'
   20 |       mon2scb.get(scb_trans);
      |                   ^~~~~~~~~~~~~
%Error: /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv:65:20: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                 : ... note: In instance 'std::mailbox__Tz1'
   65 |          message = m_queue.pop_front();
      |                    ^~~~~~~
        /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv:2:1: ... note: In file included from 'verilated_std.sv'
%Error: /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv:71:23: Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced
                                                                 : ... note: In instance 'std::mailbox__Tz1'
   71 |             message = m_queue.pop_front();
      |                       ^~~~~~~
        /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_std.sv:2:1: ... note: In file included from 'verilated_std.sv'
%Error: Exiting due to 12 error(s)
```

Which suffice to this:

`Unsupported: Signals used via read-write reference cannot be forced`

This comes when I used these in my testbench:

```
typedef enum logic [3:0] {
        IDLE = 4'b0000,
        CHECK2 = 4'b0011,
        CHECK3 = 4'b1111,
        ACCESS = 4'b1001
                //WRONG_STATE = 4'b1001
    } state_t;

 initial begin
        force dut.next_state = state_t'(4'b1010);

        release dut.next_state;
  end

```

Note that my IP is instantiated as DUT in the testbench, and the testbench components are added by instantiating test.

The `force/release` keyword only works when I used flat testbench rather than the current class based one. Meaning only when I disabled my whole tree of class based components and just pushed values through the testbench module only.

I understand the multidriven issue and I tried making sure I dont force in any pin that is already driven by another signal by introducing mux when forcing. But it did not work, ending up in the same error as above.

Am I missing something here?

(Please comment for further clarification if necessary)
